data.title: nCino, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
nCino, Inc., a leading provider of cloud-based banking solutions, has released its latest Form 10-Q report, showcasing robust financial and operational performance for the three months ended October 31, 2025. The report highlights significant growth in revenues, improved profitability, and strategic operational initiatives aimed at enhancing efficiency and expanding market presence.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenues: $152.2 million, reflecting an increase from the previous period, driven by growth in subscription services.
  • Gross Profit: $93.7 million, indicating improved efficiency in cost management.
  • Income from Operations: $11.7 million, showcasing a significant turnaround from a loss in the prior period.
  • Net Income: $8.6 million, highlighting a positive shift in profitability.
  • Net Income Per Share: $0.06, demonstrating earnings growth per share compared to a loss in the previous year.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company reported revenue from two main sources: subscription services and professional services. Subscription services are further divided into non-mortgage and mortgage segments, with non-mortgage subscriptions generating $84.5 million and mortgage subscriptions generating $21.1 million for the three months ended October 31, 2025.
  • Geographical Performance: The United States remains the largest market, contributing $118.6 million in revenue for the three months ended October 31, 2025. The United Kingdom also showed significant growth, with revenues increasing to $18.3 million, reflecting the company's expanding presence in international markets.
  • Operational Efficiency: The company implemented a Restructuring Plan in the second quarter of fiscal 2026, which included a workforce reduction of approximately 7% and office space reductions in certain markets to improve operational efficiencies.
  • Future Outlook: The company expects to recognize approximately 69% of its remaining performance obligations as revenues in the next 24 months, indicating a strong pipeline of future revenue. Additionally, the company is focused on expanding its market opportunities and achieving synergies from recent acquisitions.

SEC Filing: nCino, Inc. [ NCNO ] - 10-Q - Dec. 03, 2025

data.title: Barnwell Industries, Inc. Announces Closing of Previously Announced Private Placement Led by Bradley Radoff
data.description:
HONOLULU, HI / ACCESS Newswire / December 3, 2025 / Barnwell Industries, Inc. (NYSE American:BRN) ("Barnwell" or the "Company") today announced that it has closed its previously announced private placement of common stock and warrants raising gross proceeds of approximately $2.4 million from accredited investors.

Under the terms of the transaction, Barnwell issued an aggregate of 2.2 million shares of common stock at a purchase price of $1.10 per share. In addition, purchasers of the common stock, other than members of the Company's Board of Directors or management and one other purchaser, received warrants to purchase up to 1.0 million additional shares of common stock at an exercise price of $1.65per share, with a term of 3years (collectively, the "Securities") following a six month period during which they cannot be exercised. The Securities sold in this private placement have not been registered under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, and may not be offered or sold in the United States absent registration or an applicable exemption from registration requirements.

Philip Patman, Jr., Executive Vice President of Finance and Director, commented:

"We are pleased to complete this financing, which provides additional financial flexibility as we continue to advance operational priorities and evaluate opportunities to enhance long-term shareholder value."

The Company intends to use the net proceeds from the private placement for general corporate purposes, including strengthening its balance sheet, supporting existing operations, and pursuing strategic initiatives.

Additional details regarding the private placement are available in the Company's filings with the Securities and Exchange Commission.

Important Information

The offer and sale of the foregoing securities were made in a private placement in reliance on an exemption from the registration requirements of the Securities Act of 1933, as amended (the "Securities Act"), pursuant to Section 4(a)(2) of the Securities Act and/or Regulation D promulgated thereunder, and applicable state securities laws. Accordingly, the securities offered in the private placement may not be offered or sold in the United States absent registration with the Securities and Exchange Commission or an applicable exemption from the registration requirements of the Securities Act and such applicable state securities laws.

This press release does not constitute an offer to sell or the solicitation of an offer to buy the securities, nor shall there be any sale of the securities being offered in any state or other jurisdiction in which such offer, solicitation or sale would be unlawful prior to the registration or qualification under the securities laws of any such state or other jurisdiction.

Additional information regarding this private placement is available in a Current Report on Form 8-K filed by the Company with the Securities and Exchange Commission.

About Barnwell Industries, Inc.

Barnwell Industries, Inc. (NYSE American:BRN) is a diversified company with operations and interests in energy and related assets. The Company is focused on disciplined capital allocation, operational excellence, and high-return growth opportunities.

Forward-Looking Statements

Certain information contained in this press release contains "forward-looking statements," within the meaning of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995, Section 27A of the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, and Section 21E of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended. These forward-looking statements are based on current beliefs and expectations of our board and management team that involve risks, potential changes in circumstances, assumptions, and uncertainties, include various estimates, forecasts, projections of Barnwell's future performance, and statements of Barnwell's plans and objectives. Forward-looking statements include phrases such as "expects," "anticipates," "intends," "plans," "believes," "predicts," "estimates," "assumes," "projects," "may," "will," "will be," "should," or similar expressions. Although Barnwell believes that its current expectations are based on reasonable assumptions, it cannot assure that the expectations contained in such forward-looking statements will be achieved. Any or all of the forward-looking statements may turn out to be incorrect or be affected by inaccurate assumptions Barnwell might make or by known or unknown risks and uncertainties. These forward-looking statements are subject to risks and uncertainties including risks related to our ability to execute on our strategy and business plan and the other risks forth in the "Forward-Looking Statements," "Risk Factors" and other sections of Barnwell's Annual Report on Form 10-K for the fiscal year ended September 30, 2024 (as amended on Form-10-K/A filed on January 27, 2025) and Barnwell's other filings with the Securities and Exchange Commission. Investors should not place undue reliance on the forward-looking statements contained in this press release, as they speak only as of the date of this press release, and Barnwell expressly disclaims any obligation or undertaking to publicly release any updates or revisions to any forward-looking statements contained herein.

data.title: PLATTS: 100--New England power producers hold long-term strategies despite federal policies
data.description: (Platts)--03Dec2025/523 pm EST/2223 GMT **Offshore wind 'never getting built again' **States prioritize energy affordability Shifting federal policies are not causing power generation owners to change their long-term investment strategies, although some are seeing disruptions in the short term, executives said Dec. 2 at the New England Energy Summit in Boston. "You can't just develop and build within an administrative cycle. One has to look longer term than just what the priorities are right now," Competitive Power Ventures Inc. CEO Sherman Knight said during a panel. "Does that cause some of the assets we are developing to slow down or speed up a little? Yes, it shifts a bit. But for the most part, we are still developing and constructing renewable projects as much as natural gas." The Trump administration's "energy dominance" policy has deprioritized climate goals and seeks to boost fossil fuels. In July, Trump signed a sweeping budget reconciliation bill that accelerated the phaseout of the Inflation Reduction Act's flagship 45Y and 48E clean electricity production and investment tax credits. The administration has been particularly critical of energy policy in the US Northeast and mid-Atlantic, moving to stymie wind development off the Atlantic Coast and push for an expansion of gas pipelines in the region. Additionally, the Trump administration has delayed the retirement of coal-fired generation units. Power generation executives said this shift in federal policies has not dramatically impacted their business. "There's a lot of noise in the current administration, but I don't really see a lot of tangible things. They have kept some assets running, but I don't think they really moved the needle that much," Alpha Generation LLC CEO Curt Morgan said, acknowledging that the offshore wind industry has been impacted by federal policy changes. RENEWABLE ENERGY IMPACTS Renewable energy deals have ramped up after developers and financiers received clarity from the US Treasury Department in August on how to qualify for wind and solar credits. However, the policy environment is causing some renewable project developers to shift near-term strategies and move investment outside of the US. "On the kind of short horizon in the next few years, we are actually shifting some of our resources, and we're moving north of the border, frankly," Burlington, Massachusetts-headquartered FirstLight Power Resources Inc. CEO Justin Trudell said. "The next hundreds of millions of dollars that we invest through FirstLight are going to be in Ontario and likely in Quebec." In the US, Trudell said the company has solar projects it is looking to safe harbor under the new investment tax credit guidelines. FirstLight has about 1,600 MW of renewables in its portfolio, including the 1,168-MW Northfield Mountain pumped storage plant in Massachusetts. FirstLight has a development pipeline of 4 GW, most of which is in the 2,400-MW Leading Light Offshore Wind Project off the coast of New Jersey and New York, which the company funded. Trudell said the project is at a "complete standstill." In November, Leading Light developer Invenergy LLC told New Jersey regulators it was backing out of the project, citing cost overruns and the changing federal policy landscape. "We are riding it out until we get some better headwinds," Trudell said. ISO New England CEO Gordon van Welie said in October that offshore wind development has been set back by at least a decade. Some analysts were even more pessimistic about offshore wind development, arguing that the Trump administration's policies, which have included issuing stop-work orders to offshore wind projects under construction, have killed the industry in the US. "It's done. It's never getting built again. Unless it's a state or federal entity building it, like a Tennessee Valley Authority or New York Power Authority building the offshore wind, there's no publicly traded company that their shareholders would allow that investment," Anthony Crowdell, a managing director at Mizuho, said. "Unless we saw a federal or state entity building it, offshore wind is finished in the United States." CLIMATE POLICY COUNTERBALANCING During the first Trump administration, traditionally Democratic-led states in the Northeast advanced ambitious renewable energy and climate legislation as part of an effort to counterbalance the federal government. New York passed its landmark Climate Leadership and Community Protection Act in 2019, while Massachusetts and Rhode Island passed climate laws early into the Biden administration in 2021. "When you've got a Republican administration in Washington, DC, Democrat states, they do counterbalance things," Morgan said. "They do what they can do, especially on the environmental side. That counterbalancing that does occur does create an environment where things just don't change significantly." During the second Trump administration, however, states in the Northeast are not pursuing the same type of counterbalancing climate policies, according to analysts. Instead of ambitious climate goals, states in the Northeast have renewed their focus on energy affordability. "We think there's going to be a lot more pragmatic posture among even some of the progressive lawmakers," Timothy Fox, an analyst at Clearview Energy Partners, said. "With the prices where they are, I just think that sucks out any conversations of something else. We are already starting to see indications of that in New York, Massachusetts and elsewhere." New York's new draft energy plan charts a different path for its energy transition, including investments in nuclear energy and potentially repowering fossil fuel assets. Gov. Kathy Hochul has also said she is prioritizing energy affordability and reliability, including by asking state regulators to reject a proposed Consolidated Edison Inc. capital investment plan that included billions of dollars in energy transition-related investments. In Massachusetts, regulators have directed electric and gas utilities to trim $500 million from the state's energy efficiency program, a move criticized by environmental groups. "When they set a lot of these standards during Trump 1.0 to offset what was going on at the federal level, they did so at a time when power demand was relatively flat. In some cases, it was projected to decline because of energy efficiency needs," Fox said. "To put it very bluntly: It's a lot easier to green the grid when you are not growing. But we are growing now, so I think there's going to be very little talk about enhancing or accelerating climate goals." — Noah Schwartz, noah.schwartz@spglobal.com-- Edited by Jared Anderson, newsdesk@spglobal.com--Platts Electricity Alert--

data.title: Morgan Stanley exec expects broad surge in M&A
data.description:
By Tatiana Bautzer and Lananh Nguyen

Morgan Stanley is seeing a strong deal pipeline next year for mergers, acquisitions and IPOs, its co-head of investment banking, Mo Assomull, said on Wednesday.

M&A pipelines are broadly healthy across sectors, with technology, healthcare, industrials and financials being particularly active, he said on a panel at the Reuters NEXT conference in New York. The economic backdrop for deals is also supportive, he added.

The Trump administration's pro-growth stance is also expected to spur consolidation in the financial sector, Assomull said.

"We do expect to have more banks-related M&A over the next few years, for many reasons," he said. Those reasons include a more friendly regulatory environment and lenders' need to increase their scale.

Regarding artificial intelligence, investors and lenders have become more "discerning" about financing companies in the space, he said.

Morgan Stanley's profit beat estimates in the third quarter as a surge in dealmaking drove revenue to records. The bank's finance chief said in October that its investment banking pipeline was at "all-time highs."

View the live broadcast of the World Stage here and read full coverage here.

data.title: Supply chain software provider Tecsys' Q2 revenue hits record on SaaS strength
data.description:
Overview

  • Tecsys fiscal Q2 total revenue hits record, driven by 22% SaaS revenue growth

  • Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal Q2 rises 71% yr/yr, reaching C$5 mln

  • Company repurchased 79,714 shares under Normal Course Issuer Bid

Outlook

  • Tecsys maintains FY26 guidance for total revenue growth of 8-10%

  • Company expects FY26 SaaS revenue growth of 20-22%

  • Tecsys anticipates moderation in professional services revenue next quarter

Result Drivers

  • SAAS REVENUE - SaaS revenue increased by 22% to C$19.7 mln, driven by disciplined execution and scalability

  • PROFESSIONAL SERVICES - Professional services revenue grew 20% amid robust implementation activity, but moderation expected next quarter

  • AWS MARKETPLACE - Elite™ now available on AWS Marketplace, providing faster, trusted deployment of technology

Key Details

Metric

Beat/Miss

Actual

Consensus Estimate

Q2 Net Income

C$1.80 mln

Q2 Adjusted EBITDA

C$5 mln

Analyst Coverage

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 4 "strong buy" or "buy", no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell"

  • The average consensus recommendation for the software peer group is "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Tecsys Inc is C$48.50, about 40.6% above its December 2 closing price of C$34.50

  • The stock recently traded at 56 times the next 12-month earnings vs. a P/E of 53 three months ago

data.title: Silynxcom Withdraws Filing for a Securities Offering
Prospect of Rate Cut Lifts Shares of Smaller Companies — WSJ
data.description:
By Hannah Erin Lang

The prospect of lower interest rates lifted shares of smaller companies on Wednesday.

The Russell 2000 index of small-cap companies rose 1.9%, leading gains among major U.S. stock benchmarks. Investors expect lower rates will decrease borrowing costs and boost economic growth, lifting profits at smaller companies.

Just a few weeks ago, traders put the odds of an interest-rate cut at the Federal Reserve's Dec. 10 meeting at less than 50%. Since then, the chances have surged to nearly 90%, according to interest-rate futures tracked by CME FedWatch.

Further signs of a weakening job market juiced investors' rate-cut optimism on Wednesday: A report from payroll processor ADP showed that private hiring sank in November, with the sector shedding some 32,000 jobs.

That helped boost other indexes as well. The Dow Jones Industrial Average climbed 0.9% or around 408 points. The S&P 500 rose 0.3% and is just 0.6% off the all-time highs it notched in October.

UnitedHealth Group added 4.7% to lead the Dow industrials. Goldman Sachs gained 2.6%

But rising hopes for falling rates are hurting the U.S. dollar. The WSJ Dollar Index has fallen for seven consecutive trading days, on course for its longest losing streak since July 2020.

Lower rates generally decrease the value of a country's currency. And as U.S. investors prepare for the Fed to cut again, policymakers across the globe are taking a different path: the Bank of Japan governor signaled earlier this week that the country's central bank might raise rates later this month.

"The dollar is poised to continue weakening," said José Torres, senior economist at Interactive Brokers. "If the Fed is cutting and other banks are hiking or steady, that...weakens demand for our currency."

Investors have regained some appetite for risks after a tech-stock selloff ahead of the Thanksgiving holiday. The Nasdaq trailed behind other indexes, rising 0.2%. Bitcoin continued its recovery on Wednesday, advancing 2%.

"Inflation is benign, interest rates are trending lower and earnings are inching higher," said Terry Sandven, chief equity strategist at U.S. Bank Asset Management. "That's Goldilocks for stocks."

Shares of Walmart climbed 1.8%, and the company closed above a $900 billion market value for the first time. Last month, as the holiday season approached, the retail giant raised its forecast and said it was still winning over shoppers across the income spectrum.

Write to Hannah Erin Lang at hannaherin.lang@wsj.com

data.title: Aviat Appoints Schmidt as Finance Chief
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Aviat Networks appointed Andrew Schmidt as chief financial officer, effective immediately.

In addition to CFO, Schmidt will also serve as principal accounting officer.

Schmidt most recently was finance chief of medical aesthetics company Sientra, where he oversaw its sale in 2024. Prior to that, he was CFO of transit technology company Iteris.

He succeeds Michael Connaway, who announced his resignation in August. Andrew Fredrickson filled the CFO role in the interim.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

S&P MERVAL Index Ends 2.87% Higher at 3129449.22, Record Close — Data Talk
data.description:
The S&P MERVAL Index is up 87448.69 points or 2.87% today to 3129449.22

  • A new record close
  • Largest one-day point and percentage gain since Wednesday, Nov. 26, 2025
  • Up six of the past seven trading days
  • Up 85.84% from its 52-week low of 1683959.61 hit Friday, Sept. 19, 2025
  • Rose 41.20% from 52 weeks ago
  • Up 85.84% from its 2025 closing low of 1683959.61 hit Friday, Sept 19, 2025
  • Year-to-date it is up 595814.57 points or 23.52%

Source: Dow Jones Market Data, FactSet
